data.title: Wharf Holdings: To Maintain Prudent Financial Managment Amid Headwinds >0004.HK
Wall Street Journal - March 13
data.description:
The following are the top stories in the Wall Street Journal. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

- Intel named Lip-Bu Tan as its new chief executive, selecting a former board member and longtime industry executive to help turn around the beleaguered semiconductor company.

- Sunnova Energy , one of United States' largest residential solar companies, is preparing to engage with creditors to explore options, including a potential bankruptcy filing, over its roughly $8.5 billion in debt.

- Algoma Steel Group expects tariffs to hurt its finances and operations, though the company said it is too early to estimate the full extent of the effects.

- Chinese authorities summoned Walmart officials for a dressing-down this week after receiving complaints that the retailer was pressuring some Chinese suppliers to cut prices to absorb the cost of U.S. tariffs.

- Fossil Group plans to close 50 stores and cut jobs in a turnaround plan to focus on its core Fossil brand platform in a cost savings effort.

- Alimentation Couche-Tard will continue its friendly pursuit of Seven & i , executives said, sounding confident that they can overcome antitrust issues that could block the prospective acquisition in the U.S.

- A U.S. federal judge blocked most of a White House executive order punishing Perkins Coie, saying the administration had put itself at odds with the First Amendment by targeting the law firm based on President Donald Trump's dislike of its work for his political opponents.

data.title: Wharf Holdings Declares Second Interim Dividend of HK$0.20 >0004.HK
India 10-year bond yield glued to key level despite easing inflation
data.description:
Indian government bond yields were largely unchanged on Thursday, with the benchmark yield hovering around a key level as upbeat sentiment from easing inflation data was blunted by worries over escalations in the global tariff war.

The benchmark 10-year yield (IN067934G=CC) was at 6.6835% as of 10:00 a.m. IST, compared with its previous close of 6.6821%.

"Despite positive developments, traders have been unable to take the benchmark yield below the key technical level of 6.68%. We have seen the yield reversing from this point multiple times over the last one month and that seems to be playing out again," a trader with a primary dealership said.

U.S. yields rose on Wednesday on concerns about the potential inflationary impact of the global trade war, which outweighed optimism over slowing retail inflation in February.

The U.S. consumer price index rose 0.2% last month and 2.8% in the 12 months through February. Economists had forecast the CPI gaining 0.3% month-on-month and advancing 2.9% year-on-year, per a Reuters poll.

Indian bond yields eased slightly on Wednesday after domestic retail inflation eased to 3.61% last month, the lowest since July, and was also down from 4.26% in January. A Reuters poll had pegged the reading at 3.98%.

The lower reading has boosted the odds of a rate cut by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) for the second straight meeting in April.

Most market participants now expect at least 50 basis points of further reductions, with a 25-bp cut anticipated in April. Barclays said it does not rule out the possibility of a larger-than-usual easing of 35 bps.

Meanwhile, traders believe a big uptick in bond yields remains capped as the RBI bought bonds worth 500 billion rupees ($5.74 billion) on Wednesday and is set to purchase a similar quantum of debt next week.

($1 = 87.0670 Indian rupees)

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 10:00 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was up 0.3% to 74,270, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 rose 0.3% to 22,531, tracking global market trends, and as investors drew comfort from softer-than-expected inflation data in the U.S. and India.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose nearly 0.2% versus the U.S. dollar to 87.0625, in trading thinned out by a technical outage while modest gains in Asian currencies aided an upward bias.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 100.73 rupees, with the yield nearly unchanged at 6.6835%, as positive cues from lower inflation readings were offset by tariff worries.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down nearly 2 basis points at 6.105%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) declined a little over 1 bp to 5.955%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 6.40%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 6.30%.

data.title: Metallurgical of China Taps VP as Acting CFO
data.description:
Metallurgical of China appointed Bai Xiaohu, executive director and vice president, as acting chief financial officer.

The appointment followed former chief financial officer Zou Hongying's resignation as vice president and chief accountant, according to a Wednesday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The construction engineering company will complete the selection of the new CFO as soon as possible.

data.title: Market Chatter: HSBC Shifts Some Bankers to Temporary Retention in Investment Banking Overhaul
data.description:
HSBC Holdings is placing certain bankers on short-term retainers as it winds down selective investment banking units under a wider overhaul, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The British bank placed bankers on work arrangements ranging from three to six months to finish client mandates in certain cases. The news outlet reported that it is unclear how many bankers are impacted.

HSBC did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: India's Sensex Up 0.3%, Led by Industrial, Financial Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: Indian shares are higher as concerns about the country's inflation ease following slower-than-expected consumer-price growth data for February. Industrial and financial stocks are leading the gains. Tata Steel is up 1.4% and Bajaj Finserv is 0.9% higher. Among individual movers, Bharat Electronics is up 2.1% after it wins an INR24.63 billion defense contract to supply radars. Investors are focusing on India's trade data due later in the day as well as any developments in U.S. trade and foreign policies. The benchmark Sensex is up 0.3% at 74227.88. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Boustead Singapore to Dispose Of Certain Businesses to UI Holdings
data.description:
Boustead Singapore through its subsidiaries, BP Unity and BP-Real Estate Investments, agreed to dispose of certain fund and property management businesses of the group to UI Holdings, according to a late Wednesday filing.

As a consideration, UI Holdings will issue 35,505,812 new shares worth $78.6 million to Boustead Singapore, resulting in Boustead Singapore acquiring a 24.1% stake in UH Holdings.

Under the agreement, Boustead Singapore will also sell its 50% stake in Echo Base-BP Capital (Vietnam) to UI Holdings, a private real estate investor and fund manager.

In addition, the two parties will also discuss the potential sale of 3% of KTG & Boustead Industrial Logistics to UI Holdings for $2.3 million.

Furthermore, a Boustead Singapore unit will make a capital commitment of at least $11.6 million in UH Holdings.

data.title: Beng Kuang Marine Issues 3,000 New Shares Following Warrant Exercise
data.description:
Beng Kuang Marine allotted and issued 3,000 new shares pursuant to the exercise of warrants at a price of SG$0.22 per share, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

Following the issue of these shares, the number of issued shares of the company increased to 199,213,406 shares.

Shares of the company were up over 2% in recent trading.

Yuan firms on rising China bond yields; trade war in focus
data.description:
China's yuan firmed against the dollar on Thursday, underpinned by a rise in domestic bond yields as investors assessed the impact of an escalating global trade war.

Contrary to market consensus, the yuan has held steady despite higher tariffs, while government bond yields have risen rather than fallen, said Larry Hu, an economist at Macquarie.

Hu attributed recent weakness in the dollar and the success of local startup DeepSeek as reasons behind improved risk sentiment, which in turn, supports the yuan.

The dollar index , which measures the currency against a basket of six major peers, slipped 0.029% at 103.56, hovering near a 4-month low.

Meanwhile, higher bond yields underpinned the yuan.

China's 10-year bond yield has risen more than 20 basis points since February, as investors lowered their expectations for an imminent rate cut and funding conditions in the interbank market remained tight.

"However, one should be cautious in extrapolating the near-term trend, as the fundamentals remain weak. The trend of stronger yuan and higher bond yields will be more sustainable if domestic demand picks up," Hu said.

The spot yuan opened at 7.2350 per dollar and was last trading at 7.233 as of 0323 GMT, 53 pips firmer than the previous late session close, and 0.84% weaker than the midpoint.

U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday threatened further tariffs on European Union goods. This move comes as major U.S. trading partners announced they would retaliate against trade barriers already erected by him.

Trade tensions are rising and the outlook for U.S. exceptionalism could be fading, said Chang Wei Liang, currency and credit strategist at DBS, referring to the outperformance of the U.S. economy and assets in recent years.

"Even a softer February consumer price index (CPI) print could only give a muted boost to U.S. stocks," he said.

Prior to the market opening, the People's Bank of China set the midpoint rate , around which the yuan is allowed to trade in a 2% band, at 7.1728 per dollar, 711 pips firmer than a Reuters' estimate.

The offshore yuan traded at 7.2335 yuan per dollar , up about 0.11% in Asian trade.

data.title: Thailand Consumer Sentiment Drops from 8-Month High
data.description:
The University of the Thai Chamber of Commerce’s consumer confidence index fell to 57.8 in February 2025 from January's eight-month peak of 59.0.

The latest result ended the fourth straight month of improvement in the index.

data.title: Wharf Holdings: 2024 Rev Weighed by Mainland Development Properties >0004.HK
Thor Unit Applies to Convert Petroleum Exploration License to Regulated Substance License
data.description:
Thor Energy (ASX: THR) said that its unit, Go Exploration, applied to convert the petroleum exploration license 120 (PEL 120) to a regulated substance exploration license offered by the Department for Energy and Mining in South Australia, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The conversion allows exploration for regulated substances, including hydrogen and helium, addressing a prior regulatory limitation with PEL 120.

Go Exploration will now proceed to plan and execute the initial exploration phase, the filing said.

data.title: Saudi Arabia's NCLE Posts Higher Fiscal H1 Net Profit, Revenue
data.description:
National Co. for Learning and Education (SASE:4291), or NCLE, said Wednesday that net profit and revenue for the fiscal first half increased year over year.

Net profit for the six months ended Jan. 31 was 78.2 million Saudi riyals, compared with 65.1 million riyals a year ago. EPS moved to 1.82 riyals from 1.51 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed educational institution investor and operator's revenue was 327.7 million riyals, compared with 281.7 million riyals a year ago.

Nokia selected to upscale Vodafone Idea’s IP backhaul network for superior 4G and 5G experience
data.description:
Press Release

Nokia selected to upscale Vodafone Idea’s IP backhaul network for superior 4G and 5G experience

  • Nokia's trusted performance in critical network infrastructure strengthens its partnership with Vodafone Idea to deliver robust IP backhaul expansion through high-performance IP/MPLS solutions.
  • Nokia to deploy products from the 7750 SR and 7250 IXR series, upgrading Vodafone Idea’s transport network to deliver superfast speeds and unparalleled customer experience across multiple telecom circles in India.

13 March 2025

New Delhi, India – Nokia today announced that it has been selected by Vodafone Idea Limited (VIL), to enhance and expand its IP backhaul network. Under the three-year agreement, Nokia will be deploying its cutting-edge IP/MPLS solutions across multiple telecom circles in India. With emerging data-hungry services like AI and immersive gaming, as well as the continued need for business video conferencing and access to digital services, connectivity demands are always increasing. To ensure people and enterprises can live and work without interruption, this vital upgrade will strengthen and future-proof the network’s capacity and reliability.

As part of this partnership, Nokia will supply its latest portfolio of IP/MPLS products, including the 7750 SR and 7250 IXR series, to modernize VIL’s transport network. The deployment will help improve VIL’s core, aggregation and access layers, creating a robust, scalable and future ready network to handle the increasing data traffic and deliver superior connectivity for both mobility and enterprise services.

By incorporating the advanced solutions from Nokia, VIL aims to densify its network with a significant deployment of IP routers and replace its legacy networks. This upgrade will enable lower operational costs, faster deployment and seamless scalability for future technologies, ensuring a more efficient and cost-effective network infrastructure. The deployment also integrates advanced planning, design, installation, and migration services, supported by automation-based documentation. The upgraded network will address Vodafone Idea’s growing demand for high-capacity and resilient connectivity while ensuring sustainability through reduced Opex and energy-efficient solutions.

“Partnering with Nokia truly aligns with our vision to enhance customer experience and support the growth of data traffic in the country. The state-of-the-art solutions from Nokia and their proven deployment capabilities will enable us to build a future ready transport network. This collaboration reinforces our commitment to delivering superior connectivity and services to our customers,” said Jagbir Singh, CTO at Vodafone Idea Limited.

“We are delighted to partner with Vodafone Idea to enable the next phase of its network transformation. Our innovative IP/MPLS portfolio, in-depth understanding of 4G and 5G requirements and trusted performance across all network domains will ensure a high-performance network that delivers exceptional customer experiences for Vodafone Idea. This partnership underscores Nokia’s commitment to driving digitalization and supporting India’s telecom growth,” added Prashant Malkani, Head of Network Infrastructure at Nokia India.

Resources and additional information

Product Page: 7250 Interconnect Router

Product Page: 7750 Service Router

About Nokia

At Nokia, we create technology that helps the world act together.

As a B2B technology innovation leader, we are pioneering networks that sense, think and act by leveraging our work across mobile, fixed and cloud networks. In addition, we create value with intellectual property and long-term research, led by the award-winning Nokia Bell Labs, which is celebrating 100 years of innovation.

With truly open architectures that seamlessly integrate into any ecosystem, our high-performance networks create new opportunities for monetization and scale. Service providers, enterprises and partners worldwide trust Nokia to deliver secure, reliable and sustainable networks today – and work with us to create the digital services and applications of the future.

About Vodafone Idea Limited 

Vodafone Idea Limited is an Aditya Birla Group and Vodafone Group partnership. It is amongst India’s leading telecom service providers. The Company provides pan India Voice and Data services across 2G and 4G platforms. Company holds a large spectrum portfolio including mid band 5G spectrum in 17 circles and mmWave spectrum in 16 circles. To support the growing demand for data and voice, the Company is committed to delivering delightful customer experiences and contributing towards creating a truly ‘Digital India’ by enabling millions of citizens to connect and build a better tomorrow. The Company is developing infrastructure to introduce newer and smarter technologies, making both retail and enterprise customers future ready with innovative offerings, conveniently accessible through an ecosystem of digital channels as well as extensive on-ground presence. The Company’s equity shares are listed on National Stock Exchange (NSE) and the BSE in India.

Email: cordia.so@nokia.com

Nokia Press Office

Email: Press.Services@nokia.com

data.title: Wharf Holdings: Mainland Property Sales Remained Slow as Government Policy Effects Needed Time to Materialize >0004.HK
Oil slips amid macroeconomic concerns despite firm demand expectations
data.description:
Key points:
  • Macroeconomic concerns stemming from tariffs remain prevalent
  • Global demand firmer year on year and exceeded expectations, JP Morgan analysts say
  • Gasoline inventories fall more than expected, EIA data shows

Oil prices slipped on Thursday after a surge in the previous session on a larger-than-expected draw in U.S. gasoline stocks, as markets weighed macroeconomic concerns against firm near-term demand.

Brent futures fell 5 cents to $70.9 a barrel by 0426 GMT, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures shed 10 cents to $67.58 a barrel.

Both benchmarks rallied about 2% on Wednesday as U.S. government data showed tighter-than-expected oil and fuel inventories.

U.S. gasoline inventories fell by 5.7 million barrels, more than the 1.9 million-barrel draw expected by analysts, while distillate stocks also dropped more than anticipated - despite gains in crude stocks.

"Declining U.S. gasoline inventories raised expectations for a seasonal demand increase in spring, but concerns about the global economic impact of tariff wars weighed on the market," said Hiroyuki Kikukawa, chief strategist of Nissan Securities Investment.

"With strong and weak factors progressing simultaneously, it has become difficult for the market to lean decisively in one direction or the other," he added.

Donald Trump threatened on Wednesday to escalate a global trade war with further tariffs on European Union goods, as major U.S. trading partners said they would retaliate for trade barriers already erected by the U.S. president.

Trump's hyper-focus on tariffs has rattled investors, consumers and business confidence and raised U.S. recession fears.

Meanwhile, the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries said on Wednesday that Kazakhstan led a sizeable jump in February crude output by the wider OPEC+, highlighting a challenge for the producer group in enforcing adherence to agreed output targets.

Worries about fumbling jet fuel demand weighed further on markets, JP Morgan analysts said, adding that U.S. Transportation Security Administration data showed passenger volumes for March have decreased by 5% year-over-year, following stagnant traffic in February.

However, firm demand expectations limited overall market weakness.

Signs of robust U.S. demand and Ukraine's deployment of 377 drones targeting Russian energy infrastructure and military installations supported prices, said JP Morgan analysts in a client note.

"As of March 11, global oil demand averaged 102.2 million barrels per day, expanding 1.7 million barrels per day year-over-year and exceeding our projected increase for the month by 60,000 barrels per day," they added.

data.title: ISEC Healthcare Obtains Operating License from Malaysian Ministry of Health
data.description:
ISEC Healthcare obtained an operation license from the Ministry of Health Malaysia to operate an eye specialist center, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

The center will provide ambulatory care service in Selangor, Malaysia.

data.title: Palm Oil Trades Notably Higher
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures jumped around 1% to above MYR 4,500 per tonne after touching a low of MYR 4,454 in the previous session, supported by data from the Solvent Extractors' Association of India (SEA) showing that India's palm oil imports surged 35.7% from the prior month to 373,549 metric tons in January.

Meanwhile, Malaysian Palm Oil Board's latest data showed that inventories fell for the fifth straight month to 1.51 million tons in February, the lowest since April 2023, while production dropped 4.16% to a three-year low of 1.19 million tons as floods disrupted output.

However, gains were capped by signs of declining exports, as cargo surveyors reported a 25.8%-38.3% plunge in Malaysian palm oil shipments in the first ten days of March.

In February, industry data noted shipments fell 16.27% to a four-year low of 1 million tons.

Additionally, uncertainties lingered over the impact of President Donald Trump's tariff policies on global markets, including palm oil.

data.title: Couche-Tard Doubles Down on Friendly Takeover of 7-Eleven Owner
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO-Alimentation Couche-Tard will continue its friendly pursuit of Seven & i, executives said, sounding confident that they can overcome antitrust issues that could block the prospective acquisition in the U.S.

"We are continuing to pursue a friendly, mutually agreeable transaction" with the Japanese owner of the 7-Eleven convenience-store chain, Alain Bouchard, Couche-Tard's founder and executive chairman of the board, said at a news conference in Tokyo on Thursday.

"There is a clear path to regulatory approval," he said, while expressing frustration with the limited progress on broader deal discussions with Seven & i.

Couche-Tard Chief Executive Alex Miller said he has no plan for store closures or job cuts in Japan. "I think there's a lot of ways to increase profitability besides closing stores or firing people," he said, adding that listening to franchisees is one way to do so.

In a statement released earlier this week, the Canadian owner of Circle K reiterated its commitment to the deal, adding that there is considerable uncertainty around whether Seven & i's latest business plan can deliver value to shareholders. It also expressed disappointment that the engagement with Seven & i has been mostly focused on U.S. regulatory approval.

In September, Seven & i rejected an initial $39 billion buyout bid from Couche-Tard, saying the proposal underestimated the company's value and citing a possible antitrust challenge. Couche-Tard later raised its offer to about $47 billion.

The Canadian company said this week it resubmitted a revised, yen-denominated, nonbinding proposal on Jan. 24.

In a statement on a website, the company said it remains confident that its proposal is "the most attractive for Seven & i, its customers, franchisees, and the shareholders of both companies."

The 7-Eleven parent last week named its first American chief executive, former Walmart executive Stephen Hayes Dacus, and announced a plan to split its North American convenience stores into a separate listed company by the second half of next year.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: MTM Critical Metals Teams Up With Vedanta for Alumina Recycling Technology; Shares Up 10%
data.description:
MTM Critical Metals signed a memorandum of understanding with Vedanta to apply its proprietary flash joule heating technology for Vedanta's Red Mud alumina waste recycling project, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The company said Red Mud tailings have above-ground deposits of aluminum, titanium, gallium, scandium, and rare earth elements.

Financial terms were not disclosed.

The company's shares rose 10% in recent Thursday trade.
